perturbation.original_text: 2.01. XSPA shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to display, market, promote, offer for sale and sell the Products set forth on Exhibit A in all Stores throughout the Territory. The Products shall only be sold by XSPA at the retail price of the applicable Product as set forth on Exhibit A (the "Retail Price"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Retail Price for the Products at the Stores in the Territory (i) may only be modified by mutual agreement of the Parties, and (ii) does not include any discounts, promotions or applicable sales taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.01. XSPA shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to display, market, promote, offer for sale and sell the Products set forth on Exhibit A in all Stores throughout the Territory. The Merchandise shall only be sold by XSPA at the retail price of the applicable Product as set forth on Exhibit A (the "Retail Price"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Retail Price for the Merchandise at the Stores in the Territory (i) may only be modified by mutual agreement of the Parties, and (ii) does not include any discounts, promotions or applicable sales taxes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Merchandise' introduces ambiguity. While 'Products' is defined, 'Merchandise' is not, leading to confusion about which items are covered by the Retail Price agreement. This contradicts the rest of the text where 'Products' is meant to be well-defined and linked to Exhibit A, creating an in-text contradiction about what XSPA is obligated to sell at the Retail Price.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.02. In addition to the Products, Calm shall have the right to identify up to five (5) additional products, with such products and the price thereof to be mutually agreed by the Parties, to be displayed, marketed, promoted, offered for sale and sold in the Stores in the Territory. Such products, once agreed on by both Parties and priced in accordance with the previous sentence, shall be deemed Products set forth on Exhibit A for the purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Products' with 'Merchandise', a term not defined in the agreement. The contradicted text (2.02) explicitly states that additional products agreed upon 'shall be deemed Products set forth on Exhibit A for the purposes of this Agreement'. By changing 'Products' to the undefined 'Merchandise' in 2.01, it creates ambiguity as to whether 2.02's added products are subject to the retail price agreement in 2.01, leading to an in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology, as defined in the instructions. This ambiguity directly impacts the enforceability of the retail price clause.

perturbation.original_text: 5.05. Calm or its authorized designees shall be the sole suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, Uniforms and Inserts (collectively, "Product Collateral") for each Store throughout the Territory. Calm shall use reasonable efforts to fulfill all of XSPA's requirements for Product Collateral in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event that inventory of any Product Collateral in any Store falls below fifty percent (50%) of the initial amount of such Product Collateral provided to such Store, XSPA shall use best efforts to advise Calm in sufficient detail to enable Calm to ship additional inventory of such Product Collateral to such Store. The Parties acknowledge and agree that, as between the Parties, Calm shall retain legal title to any and all Product Collateral (other than Uniforms) until sold in a Store in the Territory or otherwise disbursed in a Store in the Territory (in the case of Gifts, Inserts and Shopping Bags) in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event Calm identifies any issue with any Product Collateral making it unsuitable for use as contemplated by this Agreement or issues a recall with respect to any Product, XSPA shall promptly return any and all affected Product Collateral to Calm or its authorized designees upon receiving notice of such issue or recall.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.05. Calm or its authorized designees shall be the sole suppliers to XSPA of XSPA's requirements for Products, Gifts, Shopping Bags, Uniforms and Inserts (collectively, "Store Inventory") for each Store throughout the Territory. Calm shall use reasonable efforts to fulfill all of XSPA's requirements for Store Inventory in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event that inventory of any Store Inventory in any Store falls below fifty percent (50%) of the initial amount of such Store Inventory provided to such Store, XSPA shall use best efforts to advise Calm in sufficient detail to enable Calm to ship additional inventory of such Store Inventory to such Store. The Parties acknowledge and agree that, as between the Parties, Calm shall retain legal title to any and all Store Inventory (other than Uniforms) until sold in a Store in the Territory or otherwise disbursed in a Store in the Territory (in the case of Gifts, Inserts and Shopping Bags) in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. In the event Calm identifies any issue with any Store Inventory making it unsuitable for use as contemplated by this Agreement or issues a recall with respect to any Product, XSPA shall promptly return any and all affected Store Inventory to Calm or its authorized designees upon receiving notice of such issue or recall.
perturbation.explanation: By renaming "Product Collateral" to "Store Inventory," but keeping the reference to Product recalls as "Product", it creates a contradiction. The contract now implies that 'Store Inventory' is distinct from 'Product,' leading to uncertainty about whether XSPA is required to return items under a Product recall, as 'Store Inventory' definition includes 'Products'. The original text makes the definition of "Product Collateral" very explicit but now in the changed text, there is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.03. Calm hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless XSPA and its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees and agents from and against any and all claims, suits, alleged regulatory violations, losses, damages and costs (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to (i) any product liability or personal injury claims with respect to any Products or Product Collateral or any alleged defects in any Products or Product Collateral; (ii) any alleged action or failure to take action by Calm's employees; (iii) the use of Calm's Marks by XSPA as authorized by this Agreement; and (iv) any breach of any covenant or agreement of Calm contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text renames 'Product Collateral' to 'Store Inventory,' but retains the term 'Product' when referring to recalls. This implies a distinction between 'Store Inventory,' which now includes 'Products,' and 'Product' itself. The contradicted text, Section 10.03, refers to indemnification for claims relating to 'Products or Product Collateral'. The change introduces ambiguity: If a 'Product' recall occurs, is XSPA only required to return 'Products' that are not considered 'Store Inventory'? Or, because 'Store Inventory' encompasses 'Products', is XSPA required to return all recalled items? This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty regarding the scope of XSPA's return obligation and Calm's indemnification responsibilities, because it redefines the definition of Product Collateral to 'Store Inventory' which then causes a contradiction with the term Products. The inconsistency significantly weakens the enforceability of Section 10.03, satisfying the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8.01. XSPA shall pay to Calm on a monthly basis an amount equal to (i) fifty percent (50%) of the Retail Price for all Products sold in the Stores in the Territory during the applicable month minus (ii) fifty percent (50%) of any commission actually paid or payable to XSPA employee(s) or contractor(s) attributable to sales of such Products during such month; provided that in no event shall such commission be greater than fifteen percent (15%) of the Retail Price for the applicable Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.01. XSPA shall pay to Calm on a monthly basis an amount equal to (i) fifty percent (50%) of the Retail Price for all Products sold in the Stores in the Territory during the applicable month minus (ii) fifty percent (50%) of any bonus actually paid or payable to XSPA employee(s) or contractor(s) attributable to sales of such Products during such month; provided that in no event shall such bonus be greater than fifteen percent (15%) of the Retail Price for the applicable Product.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'commission' to 'bonus' introduces a contradiction. The contract states that 'commission' is tied to retail sales and is deducted from the payment to Calm. By changing 'commission' to 'bonus' in Section 8.01(ii), it creates uncertainty whether this 'bonus' refers to the same compensation previously described as 'commission', creating an in-text contradiction. The 'bonus' terminology makes it unclear if it applies to retail employees, and if it should be deducted from the amount of revenue that XSPA has to pay calm. The term "bonus" is more broad, and introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.06. With respect to Products sold in Stores in the Territory, XSPA shall deliver to Calm within twenty (20) days after each calendar month, a statement ("Monthly Statement") identifying (i) the total sales of each Product during said calendar month and (ii) the total commission paid to XSPA employees or contractors under Section ​8.02, which shall include a breakdown by each individual Product and Store. Each Monthly Statement shall be fully completed and signed and certified as accurate by one of XSPA's senior officers.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'commission' and how it is calculated in relation to retail sales, which directly relates to Section 8.06, which requires a Monthly Statement that includes the total commission paid to XSPA employees or contractors. By changing 'commission' to 'bonus', the changed text introduces ambiguity and a lack of clarity. The contract now uses 'bonus' where it previously used 'commission', creating uncertainty. Section 8.06 explicitly requires a breakdown of 'commission,' but the modified Section 8.01 uses 'bonus,' which makes it unclear if the bonus is related to retail sales, which could result in an in-text contradiction.
